description_long: This object is a roughly rectangular stone pit or basin with uneven, jagged edges and a weathered, earthy appearance. The interior is hollowed out, with a circular stone feature or plug set into one end, possibly serving as a drain or pedestal. The stone surface displays a mix of dark gray, brown, and ochre tones, with visible signs of erosion and discoloration from prolonged exposure to the elements. The pit's walls are irregular and slope inward, suggesting it was either carved by hand or formed through natural processes. The stone appears dense and heavy, with a coarse, gritty texture and patches of lighter and darker coloration. The overall form suggests it could serve as a water trough, planter, or ancient urban feature such as a cistern or ritual basin.
description: A weathered, hollowed stone basin with jagged edges and a circular stone feature at one end, showing earthy tones and signs of erosion.
description_view_0: The front view reveals the deep, hollowed interior and the prominent circular stone feature set into one end.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the jagged, uneven rim and the sloping interior walls, with less visibility of the circular feature.
description_view_2: From above, the shallow depth and irregular, broken edges are most apparent, with the circular feature partially visible.
description_view_3: This angled view highlights the basin's depth and the placement of the circular stone feature, as well as the color variation on the stone surface.
attribute_location: urban archaeological site or historical park
attribute_placement: embedded in the ground as a standalone feature
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and discoloration with no artificial carvings; natural weathering patterns dominate.
attribute_weathering: Heavily weathered with rough, eroded surfaces and visible staining from soil and water exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Partially sunken and likely blends with soil or stone paving, possibly surrounded by earth or sparse vegetation.
